articles.title: Europe's Deadly Heatwave: Over 10,000 People Died From Extreme Heat in June
articles.summary: A powerful heatwave that swept across Europe in late June 2026 has been linked to more than 10,000 extra deaths, according to official data from 27 countries. Scientists say this kind of extreme heat would have been almost impossible without human-caused climate change. Most of those who died — over 9,000 — were elderly people aged 65 and above. Temperature records were broken in many countries, including France, Spain, Germany and the United Kingdom. In England and Wales alone, nearly 2,200 people are thought to have died from heat in just one June heatwave. Experts warn that rising temperatures caused by global warming are making dangerous heatwaves more common and more severe every year.

lv1: How many extra deaths happened in Europe because of the heatwave in June 2026? (2026年6月的歐洲熱浪造成多少額外死亡人數？)
Which group of people was most affected by the deadly heatwave? (哪類人受熱浪影響最嚴重？)
lv2: Why do scientists say this heatwave would have been 'almost impossible' without climate change? (為甚麼科學家說沒有氣候變化，這場熱浪幾乎不可能發生？)
Why are elderly people more at risk during extreme heatwaves compared to younger people? (為甚麼老年人在極端熱浪中比年輕人面對更大風險？)
lv3: Should governments make it a legal requirement for buildings to have air conditioning or cooling systems, given that heatwaves are becoming more frequent? What are the arguments for and against? (鑑於熱浪愈來愈頻繁，政府是否應立法要求建築物安裝冷氣或降溫設備？有哪些支持和反對的論點？)

Some people argue that wealthy nations are responsible for climate change but poorer nations suffer the most. Do you agree? How should the world share the responsibility of addressing climate change? (有人認為富裕國家是氣候變化的主要成因，但貧窮國家卻承受最多後果。你同意嗎？世界應如何分擔應對氣候變化的責任？)

articles.title: Deadly Fire at Bangkok Bar Kills At Least 28 People
articles.summary: A serious fire broke out late at night on Sunday at a bar in the popular Khao San Road area of Bangkok, Thailand's capital city. At least 28 people were killed and dozens more were injured. The blaze started suddenly and spread quickly through the crowded venue, trapping many people inside. Rescue workers rushed to the scene to help survivors. Authorities have begun an investigation to find out what caused the fire and why it spread so rapidly. This tragedy has raised urgent questions about fire safety rules in entertainment venues across Thailand, and whether enough is being done to protect the public in busy nightlife areas.

lv1: Where did the deadly fire take place? (致命火災發生在哪裡？)
How many people were killed in the Bangkok bar fire? (曼谷酒吧火災造成多少人死亡？)
lv2: Why is it especially dangerous when a fire breaks out in a crowded venue like a bar or club at night? (為甚麼火災在像酒吧或夜店等人多擁擠的場所發生時特別危險？)
What kinds of fire safety measures should public entertainment venues be required to have? (公眾娛樂場所應被要求設置哪些消防安全設施？)
lv3: After tragic events like this fire, governments often promise stricter safety rules, but enforcement can be weak. Who should be responsible — the venue owners, the government, or customers themselves — for ensuring safety in public spaces? (發生此類悲劇後，政府往往承諾收緊安全規定，但執行可能不力。公共場所的安全應由場地負責人、政府還是顧客自行負責？)

Nightlife and tourism are important to Bangkok's economy, but they can also bring safety risks. How should a city balance economic development with public safety? (夜生活和旅遊業對曼谷的經濟十分重要，但也帶來安全風險。一個城市應如何在經濟發展與公眾安全之間取得平衡？)

articles.title: DSE放榜日到了！考生今日知成績，各商戶推優惠打氣
articles.summary: 香港中學文憑試（DSE）2026年成績今日（7月15日）正式公布，萬名應屆考生終於知道自己的努力成果。不少本港商戶為給考生打氣，紛紛推出放榜專屬優惠，如特惠飲食折扣等。考生須備妥相關文件，透過大學聯合招生辦法（JUPAS）等渠道了解升學選擇。DSE成績是考生升讀本地大學的重要依據，亦影響日後的升學及就業發展。大家都期盼考生能以平常心面對成績，無論結果如何，都有多種出路可供選擇。

lv1: 商戶為甚麼在放榜日推出優惠？
lv2: DSE成績對香港學生的未來有哪些影響？
除了讀大學，DSE考生還有哪些升學或發展出路？
lv3: 有人認為香港的教育制度過分著重公開試成績，令學生承受過大壓力。你認為考試成績是衡量學生能力的最佳方式嗎？應如何改善？
若你是教育政策制定者，你會如何設計一個更公平、更全面評估學生潛能的升學制度？

articles.title: 第36屆香港書展今日開幕！7天活動以文創傳承為主題
articles.summary: 第36屆香港書展今日（7月15日）在灣仔香港會議展覽中心正式揭幕，活動連續舉行七天。今屆書展以「從香港閱讀世界：文創傳承」為主題，鼓勵市民透過閱讀認識本地及國際文化。書展每年吸引大批市民及學生入場，是本港重要的文化盛事。展覽設有多個展區，包括書籍銷售、作家講座及兒童活動等，適合不同年齡人士參與。主辦單位希望透過書展推廣閱讀風氣，讓更多人愛上文字與知識。

lv1: 第36屆香港書展在哪裡舉行？
今屆書展的主題是甚麼？
lv2: 為甚麼舉辦書展有助推廣閱讀文化？書展對社會有哪些好處？
你認為書展應加入哪些活動，才能吸引更多年輕人參與？
lv3: 在數碼時代，電子書和網上資訊日趨普及，實體書展是否仍有存在的價值？請從不同角度分析。
「從香港閱讀世界」這個主題反映了甚麼樣的教育理念？閱讀對培養學生的國際視野有多重要？
